props.command: is this "Claude agentic ecosystem" wheel real?
output: the wheel: real core, invented filler
the viral fact-check: right — and wrong twice
the flagship is missing from both

beats.narration: There's a wheel going around that maps the entire Claude ecosystem — and a viral fact-check tearing it apart. Here's the fun part: they're both wrong, in mirror image. Today we grade the wheel AND the graders.

The wheel first, rebuilt box by box. Eight categories, symmetric spokes, a tidy grid of products — and that symmetry is your first tell. Real product families are never this neat. But tidy doesn't mean fake. Let's actually check.

The core holds up. Opus, Haiku, the Agent S D K, Managed Agents, Claude Code, Cowork, M C P — real and shipping. Claude Design too — a real Anthropic Labs preview, though it runs Opus four point seven, not four point eight like the wheel says.

The stale ring. Sonnet four point six on the wheel — it's Sonnet five now. Mythos still invite-only, no public rollout announced. And the miss almost nobody caught: Fable five isn't on the wheel at all. The flagship — missing from the map of its own ecosystem.

Now the invented ring — thirteen boxes. Claude Context Store. Safe Artifact Repo. A Constitutional A I checker you can apparently buy. None of these exist as products. They're features and training methods wearing product costumes, invented to complete the pattern. The fact-check called all of this correctly.

props.question: What's the debunker's mirror-image mistake?
props.commit: commit — the twist is one beat away

beats.narration: So the fact-check wins? Not yet. If the wheel's sin is inventing products to complete a pattern — predict the mirror-image sin a debunker commits. Commit to it.

It un-invents them. Claude Marketplace — filed as fake — is a real product; enterprises buy partner tools there today. Claude Security — also filed as fake — real, shipping. 'Sounds like template filler' is not a source. Both sides failed exactly the same way: pattern-confidence outrunning verification.

artifactLines: The wheel: real core (models · SDK · Code · Cowork · MCP · Design) — stale models ring, 13 invented boxes.
Missing from the wheel entirely: Claude Fable 5 — the flagship.
The fact-check: right on the filler, wrong twice — Marketplace and Security are real, shipping products.
The rule: a fact-check that doesn't cite sources is just a second opinion.

beats.narration: The verdict, both directions. The wheel: real core, stale models, thirteen invented boxes, and the flagship missing entirely. The fact-check: right about the filler, wrong about two real products. One rule falls out of this episode: a fact-check that doesn't cite sources is just a second opinion.

props.command: Here's an infographic I want checked. For every box: real product, real feature dressed as a product, or invented? Cite a primary source for each verdict — no source, no verdict.

beats.narration: Your turn — run this on the next viral diagram in your feed, and hold one line: no source, no verdict. Claude will show receipts, box by box.
Claude, debunked — question mark. If this saved you a reshare, subscribe. Nik Bear Brown.
